provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Non-Governmental Regulations, 2004.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: " Act " means the Non-Governmental Organizations Act, 2002; " Board " means the Non-Govemmental Organizations Coordination Board; " Public Officer " means a public officer appointed under the Act ; and " Registrar " means the Director for Non-Govemmental Organizations Coordination. Part II – Various prescribed Forms

provisions.title: Forms
provisions.content: The Forms in the First Schedule hereto shall be used in all matters to which they refer, or are capable of being applied or adopted with such alterations and additions, if any, as are necessary or desired for the better carrying out of the provisions of the Non-Governmental Act .

provisions.title: Docu­ments for which no form is prescribed
provisions.content: The documents for which no forms is provided or to which the prescribed forms cannot conveniently be adopted shall be in such form as the Director shall direct.

provisions.title: Document to be in Kiswahili or English
provisions.content: (1) All applications, returns and notifications shall be clearly and legibly written, printed or typed in English or Kiswahili language. (2) Where any document required to be annexed to the application or for incorporation is in any language other then English or Kiswahili, there shall be furnished therewith a verified translation of the same in English or Kiswahili (3) Any document or translated document as per sub-regulations (2) of this regulations, shall be verified by consular, experts, or authorized persons acquainted or conversant with the language to be translated and shall be certified as true copy of the original by an advocate or notary public or any other person so authorized as the case may be, and approved by the Director certifying the same to be a true copy or translation of the original. Part III – Registration appeals and inspection

provisions.title: Signatory to the docu­ments
provisions.content: (1) Every application for incorporation or compliance shall be signed by the person or group of persons who wish to apply for registration or compliance of a Non-Governmental Organization as the case may be. (2) Any other application, returns or notification, shall be signed by one or more persons, as the case may be.

provisions.title: Procedure for registration by a Public Officer
provisions.content: (1) A person or group of persons, being founder member or members, who wish to apply for registration of Non-Governmental Organization in the Region or District, shall make an application in the prescribed form to the Public officer within the District or Region. (2) The Public Officer shall within twenty one days after receiving an application for registration— (a) grant application; or (b) refuse to grant the application. (3) Where a Public Officer has granted registration as per sub­regulation (2) of this regulation shall make a report to that effect and submit it to the Director. (4) Where a Public Officer has refused the application for registration, he shall within fourteen days inform the applicant of his refusal and grounds thereof and shall make a report to that effect and submit it to the Director.

provisions.title: Appeal
provisions.content: (1) Where the Applicant is aggrieved by the decision of a Public Officer may appeal to the Director. (2) The Director within thirty days after receiving the report pursuant to sub-regulation (3) and (4) of this regulation shall have powers to vary or uphold or seek further particulars from either the Public Officer or the applicant. (3) Where the applicant is aggrieved by the decision of the Director, he may appeal to the Board accordingly. (4) Where the Applicant is aggrieved by the decision of the Board he may appeal to the Minister. (5) Every application for Review or Appeal shall be presented by a memorandum.

provisions.title: Inspection of NGO's documents under the custodian of the Registrar
provisions.content: (1) Where a member of the public wishes to exercise his right of access to an inspect any documents of any Non-Governmental Organization at the Office of the Director, he shall make the application in writing, specifying the documents he so wishes to access or inspect and the reasons thereof to the Director. (2) The Director, after receiving the application, and find out that there is sufficient cause for the Applicant to access or inspect any documents may allow the access and inspection. (3) Any member of Public who wishes to access or inspect any document shall access and inspect upon payment of the fees as prescribed in the second schedule of these Regulations.

provisions.title: Fees
provisions.content: The fees set out in the Second Schedule hereto shall be charged for the several matters set out in that schedule. First Schedule (Made under section 12(1) of Act No. 24 2002) Forms [Please note: Forms have not been reproduced. Please refer to the publication document.] Second Schedule (Made under regulation (10)) 1. Fees and expenses for registration of Non-Governmental Organizations in terms of regulation 10 shall bee as follows: (1) (a) Filing fees Shillings 15,000/= (b) Registration fees Shillings 25,000/= (c) Stamp duty Shillings 1,500/= (2) The fees shall be payable by a person or group of persons who wish to apply for registration of a Non-Governmental Organization may as the case be. 2. The fees payable on lodging any document shall be payable at the time when the document is lodged to the Registrar or Authorised officer. 3. Any member of public wishes to inspect the Non-Governmental documents Organizations in the office of the Registrar shall pay perusal fee not less than 5,000/= per each perusal.
